title: How Paige Johnson Built a Pilates Empire While Managing Motherhood
guestBio: Paige Johnson is the co-owner of Mantra Pilates, a former competitive gymnast, and beloved gymnastics coach. Known for her powerful presence and compassionate leadership, she has built a thriving fitness community while navigating the demands of motherhood and entrepreneurship.
excerpt: Paige Johnson reveals the invisible work behind building a successful Pilates studio while raising a daughter. She shares how movement became her form of leadership and the tech tools that keep her grounded in both business and life.
metaDescription: Pilates studio owner Paige Johnson shares how she manages business, motherhood, and personal purpose through movement and mindful leadership strategies.
keyTakeaways: The invisible labor of running a business requires intentional systems and boundaries to manage effectively.
Women in fitness and business spaces are often underestimated, but this can become a source of strength and motivation.
Movement is not just physical fitness—it's a form of emotional and energetic leadership that impacts every area of life.
Technology tools can help busy mothers stay organized and connected while managing multiple responsibilities.
Building community through shared purpose creates deeper connections than traditional networking approaches.
Personal mantras and grounding rituals are essential for maintaining clarity during challenging seasons.
Rising up despite doubt requires both inner strength and the grace to let go of perfectionism.

pullQuotes.text: Movement isn't just fitness — it's emotional and energetic leadership

timestamps.label: Paige's journey from gymnastics to Pilates
The invisible work of running a studio

content:
What does it really take to build a thriving fitness business while raising a daughter and staying true to your personal mission? Paige Johnson, co-owner of Mantra Pilates, knows this balancing act intimately. In this powerful conversation with Aimee Rickabus, Paige opens up about the invisible work that keeps both studios and families running, and how she's learned to lead through movement in every area of her life.

Paige's journey from competitive gymnast to beloved coach to studio co-owner wasn't a straight line. Like so many women in leadership, she's had to navigate being underestimated in business spaces while proving her worth through results rather than recognition. But what makes Paige's story particularly compelling is how she's transformed these challenges into sources of strength, using her experience to build something bigger than herself.

The Invisible Architecture of Success

Running a Pilates studio might look like it's all about the classes, the instructors, and the beautiful space. But as Paige reveals, there's an entire invisible infrastructure that keeps everything flowing. From scheduling and staff management to client relationships and business development, the behind-the-scenes work is where real leadership happens. This resonates deeply with themes from Chapter 1 of The Manage Her, particularly around the unseen labor that women consistently perform to keep their worlds functioning.

What's fascinating about Paige's approach is how she's learned to make the invisible visible, both for herself and her team. She doesn't try to do everything alone or pretend that running a business is effortless. Instead, she's created systems and boundaries that allow her to manage the complexity without burning out. This kind of honest leadership creates space for other women to be real about their own challenges.

Movement as Leadership Philosophy

For Paige, Pilates isn't just about physical fitness—it's a metaphor for how she approaches leadership in every area of her life. Movement requires presence, intentionality, and the ability to adjust when something isn't working. These same principles guide how she runs her business, parents her daughter, and maintains her own well-being. When you understand movement as both physical and energetic leadership, it changes how you show up in the world.

This perspective shift is particularly powerful for women who might not see themselves as traditional leaders. You don't need a corner office or a formal title to practice leadership. Every time you guide a class, support a team member, or create space for someone else to grow, you're leading. Paige has built her entire business model around this understanding, creating a community where leadership happens through connection and shared purpose rather than hierarchy.

Technology That Actually Serves You

One of the most practical elements of this conversation centers on the tech tools that help Paige stay organized and connected without becoming overwhelmed. As busy women, we're constantly told about new apps and systems that promise to revolutionize our productivity. But Paige's approach is refreshingly selective. She focuses on tools that genuinely serve her goals rather than adding complexity to her life.

The key is choosing technology that aligns with how you actually work and live, not how productivity gurus think you should work. For Paige, this means prioritizing tools that help her maintain relationships, track what matters most, and create space for the spontaneous moments that make life meaningful. This kind of intentional approach to technology is especially important for mothers and business owners who are already managing multiple demands on their attention.

The Strength to Rise and the Grace to Release

Perhaps the most powerful theme in this conversation is the balance between rising up when the world doubts your strength and having the grace to let some things go. Paige has learned that holding everything perfectly is not only impossible but counterproductive. Real leadership requires knowing when to push forward and when to release control, when to fight for what matters and when to trust the process.

This wisdom comes from years of navigating the unique pressures that women face in business and motherhood. Society often expects women to be everything to everyone while making it look effortless. Paige's approach challenges this narrative by showing what it looks like to be strong and vulnerable, ambitious and grounded, successful and human all at the same time.

Building Community Through Authentic Connection

What sets Paige apart as a leader is her understanding that community isn't built through networking events or social media strategies—it's created through authentic connection and shared experiences. At Mantra Pilates, she's fostered an environment where people come not just for the workout but for the sense of belonging and support they find there.

This kind of community building requires vulnerability and consistency. It means showing up authentically even when you're having a challenging day, creating space for others to do the same, and recognizing that your role as a leader is often to hold space for transformation rather than directing every outcome. For women looking to build their own communities, whether in business or other areas of life, Paige's approach offers a roadmap for creating connections that truly matter.

As this conversation demonstrates, managing it all isn't about perfection—it's about presence, intention, and the courage to keep growing. Paige Johnson's story reminds us that leadership comes in many forms, and sometimes the most powerful thing you can do is create space for others to discover their own strength through movement, community, and authentic connection.
